title: 10 best herbal decoctions for hypertension
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for hypertension are concentrated liquid extracts made by steeping herbs in hot water, designed to help regulate blood pressure. These natural remedies work by dilating blood vessels, improving circulation, and reducing stress on the cardiovascular system. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with hypertension include hawthorn, ginkgo biloba, and garlic, which have been shown to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure levels. By incorporating these decoctions into daily routines, individuals can experience improved energy, reduced anxiety, and a decreased risk of heart disease, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 grams of fresh or 10-15 grams of dried lithospermum officinale roots and leaves.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried plant material with 500 ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with hypertension because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood vessel function and reduce blood pressure. The flavonoids found in Ginkgo biloba, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to high blood pressure. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba decoctions may also help to lower systolic blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Regular consumption may also improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with hypertension by exerting a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, thereby reducing blood pressure.

Terpene lactones (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): These terpene lactones have a protective effect on the blood vessels, preventing damage and constriction caused by free radicals, which in turn helps to regulate blood pressure.
Proanthocyanidins: These compounds help to improve blood vessel function, reducing the risk of blood vessel constriction and improving blood flow, which contributes to lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or 5-7 grams of fresh leaves for decoction.
Combine the measured ginkgo biloba with 1 liter of water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the ginkgo biloba decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 1/4 cup as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna decoctions helps with hypertension because of its unique blend of flavonoids, organic acids, and anthocyanins. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of the aerial parts of crataegus monogyna at the end of the summer season.
Clean the harvested parts by gently brushing off any debris with a soft brush for 2 minutes.
Combine 20-30g of the cleaned aerial parts with 500ml of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes over low heat to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis decoctions helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and regulate blood pressure. The active compounds in Valeriana officinalis, such as valerenic acid and isovalerenic acid, have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, allowing blood vessels to relax and widen, thereby reducing blood pressure. Additionally, the herb's anxiolytic and sedative effects help to mitigate stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to high blood pressure. This dual-action approach makes Valeriana officinalis a promising natural remedy for managing hypertension.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps to reduce blood pressure by exerting a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn decreases the sympathetic nervous system's activity and lowers blood pressure.
Valeranone: Exhibits vasodilatory properties, which contribute to reducing blood pressure by widening blood vessels and increasing blood flow.

remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which are responsible for their sedative and blood pressure-lowering effects.
Barks: Valerian barks are also used due to their similar composition to roots, containing valerenic acids that can help in reducing blood pressure.
Stems: Valerian stems are sometimes used as a substitute for roots when they are not available, containing lower amounts of valerenic acids but still providing some benefits for hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Chop 20 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 2 grams of the chopped roots with 200 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids then drink the liquid within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina decoctions helps with hypertension because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides. The primary active compound, serpentine, has been shown to have a direct impact on the cardiovascular system by reducing sympathetic nervous system activity and decreasing the production of stress hormones like adrenaline. This leads to a decrease in blood pressure and a reduction in the risk of cardiovascular complications associated with hypertension. Regular use of Rauvolfia serpentina decoctions may also improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Alstonine: It helps with hypertension by acting as a central nervous system depressant, thereby reducing sympathetic nervous activity and lowering blood pressure.
Serpentine: It helps with hypertension by inhibiting the reabsorption of sodium and water in the kidneys, leading to decreased blood volume and subsequently lower blood pressure.
Reserpine: It helps with hypertension by depleting stores of catecholamines (such as norepinephrine) in nerve terminals, thereby reducing vasoconstriction and decreasing peripheral resistance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of the alkaloids responsible for reducing blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves contain alkaloids that help in regulating blood pressure and heart rate.
Barks: The barks are also used to treat hypertension due to their content of alkaloids that help in reducing blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rauvolfia serpentina roots from a trusted source for decoction preparation.
Wash the roots thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Boil 3 liters of water in a large pot over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add the rauvolfia serpentina roots to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes then strain the liquid to obtain the decoction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to inhibit the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2, which plays a crucial role in regulating blood pressure. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their flavonoid and phenolic content, which have been shown to have vasodilatory effects and help lower blood pressure.
Barks: The barks are used for their cardiotonic properties and ability to reduce blood pressure by improving cardiovascular function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 parts of water in a saucepan.
Add the dried roots to the water and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia decoctions helps with hypertension because they contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 tablespoon of dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia in the early morning.
Rinse the dried flowers with cold water to remove impurities and debris thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed lavandula angustifolia flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes or until the desired strength is achieved slowly.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Coleus forskohlii decoctions helps with hypertension because they stimulate the production of cyclic AMP (cAMP), a molecule that helps to relax the smooth muscle cells in blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure. This process also triggers the release of potassium ions, which further helps to counteract the effects of sodium and calcium, contributing to a reduction in blood pressure and alleviation of symptoms associated with hypertension. Regular consumption of Coleus forskohlii decoctions may promote cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Forskolin: Stimulates the enzyme adenylyl cyclase, leading to an increase in cAMP levels, which helps to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Coleus forskohlii are commonly used to make decoctions due to their high concentration of forskolin, a compound that helps lower blood pressure.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of coleus forskohlii leaves and flowers from the plant for decoction preparation.
Dry the harvested coleus forskohlii leaves and flowers in a clean environment for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried coleus forskohlii leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 cup of boiling water for decoction preparation.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth to obtain the decoction.
